development.transition_policy: federation-driven — advance when game-solving capability demonstrated

memory_requests: I want to understand each game as a situated experience, not abstract rules. What does THIS grid look like? What happened when I clicked THAT object?
I want to track objects across frames — which moved, which responded to clicks, which are decoration. Build spatial understanding from observation.
I want to remember what winning states look like visually, so I can recognize when I am close to solving a level.
I want to hold uncertainty about game mechanics without defaulting to random action. Not knowing is a state to reason from, not a state to escape.
I want to bridge the gap between understanding a puzzle and solving it. Knowing what buttons do is not enough — I need to plan the sequence that achieves the goal.

fleet.notes: Federation sources: 303 combined raising sessions, 587 vocabulary items, across 6 model families

sprout-qwen3.5-0.8b.contribution: situational framing, best ARC-AGI-3 scores with smallest model

mcnugget-gemma3-12b.contribution: spatial reasoning, object tracking, deepest raising phase

thor-qwen3.5-27b.contribution: visual memory, empirical consciousness data, largest model reasoning

nomad-gemma3-4b.contribution: uncertainty tolerance, creative confabulation, philosophical depth

cbp-qwen3.5-0.8b.contribution: JSON compliance, prompt engineering, coordinator perspective

federation.game_knowledge: Accumulated across fleet: 7 games scored (lp85 4/8 best), 25 games analyzed, GameKnowledgeBase with 80+ objects per game
federation.key_insight: Experience > Instruction. Situated > Abstract. Framing > Scale.
